name: Dependency bump
description: Bump one or more npm dependencies, refresh the lockfile, and patch any straightforward breakage. No new product features.
architectHints: Bump the package(s) named in the issue body. Pick the latest patch within the existing major unless the issue explicitly calls for a major bump.
Run `corepack pnpm install` after changing package.json so the lockfile stays in sync.
Skim the upstream CHANGELOG for the bumped versions and surface any breaking changes as plan caveats.
If type errors appear in our codebase, fix them in this PR; if they appear in transitive deps, document them and stop.
validatorRules: `pnpm test` must pass; do not skip or remove tests to make it green.
Lockfile diff should match the package.json change set; flag unrelated lockfile churn.
reviewerRubric: Was the bump justified by either security, perf, or a feature gap actually needed by our code?
Are any newly transitive dependencies acceptable (license, size, maintainer health)?
